title: Of the Quest of the Golden Fleece
venue: A. C. McClurg and Co., Chicago

license.note: Published 1903; public domain in the United States.

abstract: Of the Quest of the Golden Fleece (1903), by W. E. B. Du Bois. From The Souls of Black Folk.
excerpt: But the Brute said in his breast, “Till the mills I grind have ceased, The riches shall be dust of dust, dry ashes be the feast! “On the strong and cunning few Cynic favors I will strew; I will stuff their maw with overplus until...
